macro_events.event_summary: Oil prices extend rally with Brent topping $115/bbl after Trump issues new threat to Iran, raising Middle East supply risk premium.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium on crude from potential Iran sanctions/conflict
Higher realized crude prices boosting upstream cash flow
Higher fuel input costs squeezing transport/consumer margins

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream production (~3.7M boe/d); each $10/bbl move in Brent flows directly to upstream EBITDA. Brent at $115 is well above marginal cost, driving FCF and buyback expansion.
Similar pure-play integrated leverage to Brent; ~50%+ upstream earnings mix. Permian and Gulf assets monetize directly at higher crude realizations.
Highest-beta US large-cap E&P to oil price due to Permian concentration and residual debt load — operating leverage amplifies move. Buffett-backed positioning attracts flows on oil rallies.
Direct geopolitical exposure — operates in Egypt and has had North Sea/Suriname assets; high beta to Brent specifically (not just WTI) given international production mix.
Oilfield services with outsized international/Middle East revenue exposure (~60% intl); sustained $100+ Brent triggers capex upcycle and pricing power on completions.
Permian pure-play with low breakevens (~$40); $115 Brent translates to massive FCF yield and variable dividend uplift.
Cheniere benefits from oil-linked LNG contracts in Asia and wider TTF/Henry Hub spreads as European buyers price in Middle East supply risk to all hydrocarbons.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs; airlines have limited near-term hedging at $115 Brent and weak pricing power to fully pass through. Direct margin compression.
Same jet fuel cost structure as DAL; international long-haul routes especially sensitive given fuel-burn intensity. Consensus EPS at risk on sustained crude rally.
Southwest historically hedges but exposure remains; domestic leisure demand also softens as gasoline prices follow crude higher, hitting both cost and revenue sides.
Fuel surcharges lag spot diesel/jet by weeks; ground and Express segments see margin compression on rapid crude spikes before pricing catches up.
Bunker fuel is a major cost line for cruise operators; $115 Brent meaningfully pressures yields just as discretionary travel demand may also weaken on consumer fuel inflation.
Naphtha/ethane feedstocks track crude; commodity chemicals face margin squeeze as input costs rise faster than they can pass through to industrial buyers.
Higher gasoline prices act as a tax on low/middle-income consumers — Walmart's core demographic — pressuring discretionary general merchandise mix even as grocery holds.

headlines: Oil prices extend multi-day rally as Trump issues new threat to Iran; Brent tops $115 per barrel
